title: Cynorkis pleistadenia
taxonomy: Cynorkis pleistadenia belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristic orchid-like flowers and growth habits. The genus Cynorkis is a group of orchids that are native to tropical regions, particularly in Africa. The classification of Cynorkis pleistadenia as an orchid is supported by its morphological and anatomical features.

intro: Cynorkis pleistadenia is a species of orchid native to tropical regions of Madagascar, characterized by its small, delicate flowers and unique growth habits. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, coughs, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
